paragraphs.en: Each morning, I walk the same path through the park. The trees stand like silent guardians, their leaves whispering in the breeze. I have taken this route for years, yet each day offers a new shade of light or a different bird's song. It is a ritual that grounds me before the day begins.
paragraphs.zh: 每天早晨，我都沿着公园里同一条小路散步。树木宛如沉默的卫士，枝叶在微风中低语。这条路我已走了多年，但每天都有新的光影变化或不同的鸟鸣。这是一种仪式，让我在一天开始前安定下来。

vocabulary.example: Morning coffee is a ritual for many people.
vocabulary.derivatives: ritual (n.) → ritualistic (adj.) 仪式性的 → ritually (adv.) 仪式性地

vocabulary.example: The wind whispered through the trees.
vocabulary.derivatives: whisper (v.) → whispered (adj.) 低语的 → whispering (n.) 低语声

vocabulary.example: Reading poetry grounds me in difficult times.
vocabulary.derivatives: ground (v.) → grounded (adj.) 稳重的 → grounding (n.) 基础训练

chunks.en: Each morning, I walk the same path through the park.
chunks.zh: 每天早晨，我都沿着公园里同一条小路散步。
chunks.function: 主句（主语+谓语+地点状语）

chunks.en: The trees stand like silent guardians, their leaves whispering in the breeze.
chunks.zh: 树木宛如沉默的卫士，枝叶在微风中低语。
chunks.function: 并列句（独立主格结构作伴随状语）

chunks.en: I have taken this route for years, yet each day offers a new shade of light or a different bird's song.
chunks.zh: 这条路我已走了多年，但每天都有新的光影变化或不同的鸟鸣。

chunks.en: It is a ritual that grounds me before the day begins.
chunks.zh: 这是一种仪式，让我在一天开始前安定下来。

difficult_sentences.en: The trees stand like silent guardians, their leaves whispering in the breeze.
difficult_sentences.zh: 树木宛如沉默的卫士，枝叶在微风中低语。
difficult_sentences.grammar: 独立主格结构：名词+现在分词短语，表示伴随状态。
difficult_sentences.pattern: noun + present participle phrase (独立主格结构)
difficult_sentences.note: 此结构用于描述生动场景，省去连词，多用于文学或正式文体。

points.explanation: 由“名词+现在分词”构成，表示伴随情况或原因，通常用于书面语，使句子更简洁。
points.example_sentence: The trees stand like silent guardians, their leaves whispering in the breeze.
points.common_mistake: ❌ The trees stand, their leaves are whispering → ✅ The trees stand, their leaves whispering (避免使用完整从句)
points.contrast: 与with结构对比：'with their leaves whispering'更口语化，独立主格更正式、文雅。

paragraphs.en: One morning, I noticed an elderly man sitting on a bench. He wore a faded coat and held a book, but his eyes were closed, his face turned towards the sun. I wondered what memories lingered behind his peaceful expression. Perhaps he was not reading the book, but the morning itself.
paragraphs.zh: 一天早晨，我注意到一位老人坐在长椅上。他穿着一件褪色的外套，手里拿着一本书，但眼睛闭着，脸朝向太阳。我好奇他平静的表情背后藏着怎样的回忆。也许他读的不是书，而是清晨本身。

vocabulary.meaning: adj. 年老的；上了年纪的
vocabulary.example: An elderly couple walked slowly hand in hand.
vocabulary.derivatives: elder (n./adj.) → elderly (adj.) → eldership (n.) 长辈身份

vocabulary.example: The smell of coffee lingered in the room.
vocabulary.derivatives: linger (v.) → lingering (adj.) 缠绵的 → lingerer (n.) 逗留者

vocabulary.example: The photograph was faded with age.

chunks.en: One morning, I noticed an elderly man sitting on a bench.
chunks.zh: 一天早晨，我注意到一位老人坐在长椅上。

chunks.en: He wore a faded coat and held a book, but his eyes were closed, his face turned towards the sun.
chunks.zh: 他穿着一件褪色的外套，手里拿着一本书，但眼睛闭着，脸朝向太阳。

chunks.en: I wondered what memories lingered behind his peaceful expression.
chunks.zh: 我好奇他平静的表情背后藏着怎样的回忆。

chunks.en: Perhaps he was not reading the book, but the morning itself.
chunks.zh: 也许他读的不是书，而是清晨本身。

difficult_sentences.en: Perhaps he was not reading the book, but the morning itself.
difficult_sentences.zh: 也许他读的不是书，而是清晨本身。
difficult_sentences.grammar: not...but...并列结构，连接两个名词短语作宾语。
difficult_sentences.note: 此结构强调对比，B 是真正的重点；注意前后成分平行。

points.explanation: 连接两个平行成分，表示“不是…而是…”。常用于纠正或对比。
points.example_sentence: Perhaps he was not reading the book, but the morning itself.
points.common_mistake: ❌ He not read the book but the morning → ✅ He was not reading the book but the morning itself (保持动词时态一致)
points.contrast: 与rather than对比：rather than后接动名词形式，如'…rather than reading the book'，语气更正式。

paragraphs.en: His stillness taught me a lesson. In our busy lives, we often hurry past the present, clutching our phones and schedules. Yet here was a man who had mastered the art of being still, of letting the world come to him. It is a skill I long to learn.
paragraphs.zh: 他的静止给我上了一课。在忙碌的生活中，我们常常匆匆掠过当下，紧握手机和日程表。而眼前这位老人却掌握了静默的艺术，让世界向他走来。这是我渴望学会的本领。

vocabulary.example: The stillness of the forest was broken by a bird's call.
She mastered the technique after years of practice.
vocabulary.derivatives: master (v./n.) → mastery (n.) 精通 → masterful (adj.) 技艺高超的

vocabulary.example: Cooking is an art that requires creativity.
vocabulary.derivatives: art (n.) → artistic (adj.) 艺术的 → artist (n.) 艺术家

chunks.en: His stillness taught me a lesson.
chunks.zh: 他的静止给我上了一课。
chunks.function: 主句（主语+谓语+间接宾语+直接宾语）

chunks.en: In our busy lives, we often hurry past the present, clutching our phones and schedules.
chunks.zh: 在忙碌的生活中，我们常常匆匆掠过当下，紧握手机和日程表。

chunks.en: Yet here was a man who had mastered the art of being still, of letting the world come to him.
chunks.zh: 而眼前这位老人却掌握了静默的艺术，让世界向他走来。
chunks.function: 倒装句（定语从句，并列介词短语）

chunks.en: It is a skill I long to learn.
chunks.zh: 这是我渴望学会的本领。
chunks.function: 主句含省略关系词的定语从句

difficult_sentences.en: Yet here was a man who had mastered the art of being still, of letting the world come to him.
difficult_sentences.zh: 而眼前这位老人却掌握了静默的艺术，让世界向他走来。
difficult_sentences.grammar: here引导的倒装句，定语从句包含两个并列的of介词短语。
difficult_sentences.pattern: Here + be + noun + 定语从句
difficult_sentences.note: 倒装句强调地点或引入新信息；定语从句中的介词短语并列，表示同一领域的不同方面。

points.explanation: 将'here'置于句首引起倒装，正常语序为'Yet a man was here...'，倒装后更强调这人的出现。
points.example_sentence: Yet here was a man who had mastered the art of being still, of letting the world come to him.
points.common_mistake: ❌ Yet here a man was → ✅ Yet here was a man (主谓倒装)
points.contrast: 与there be句型对比：there be表存在，here be强调位置或引入具体人物。

paragraphs.en: Since that morning, I have tried to incorporate stillness into my routine. I now pause occasionally on my walks, closing my eyes and feeling the sun. I may not fully master the art, but I have begun. And perhaps that is enough.
paragraphs.zh: 从那天早晨起，我试着将静默融入日常。现在散步时，我会偶尔停下，闭上眼睛感受阳光。也许我无法完全掌握这门艺术，但我已经开始了。或许这就足够了。

vocabulary.example: We need to incorporate these changes into the plan.
vocabulary.derivatives: incorporate (v.) → incorporation (n.) 纳入 → incorporated (adj.) 合并的

vocabulary.example: He paused for a moment to catch his breath.
vocabulary.derivatives: pause (v./n.) → pausal (adj.) 停顿的 → pauser (n.) 暂停者

vocabulary.example: Is the food enough for everyone?
vocabulary.derivatives: enough (adj./adv.) → suffice (v.) 足够 → sufficiency (n.) 充足

chunks.en: Since that morning, I have tried to incorporate stillness into my routine.
chunks.zh: 从那天早晨起，我试着将静默融入日常。

chunks.en: I now pause occasionally on my walks, closing my eyes and feeling the sun.
chunks.zh: 现在散步时，我会偶尔停下，闭上眼睛感受阳光。

chunks.en: I may not fully master the art, but I have begun.
chunks.zh: 也许我无法完全掌握这门艺术，但我已经开始了。

difficult_sentences.en: I may not fully master the art, but I have begun.
difficult_sentences.zh: 也许我无法完全掌握这门艺术，但我已经开始了。
difficult_sentences.grammar: may not...but结构，表示让步转折。
difficult_sentences.pattern: may not + 动词，but + 肯定句
difficult_sentences.note: 此结构表达尽管有局限，但仍有积极进展；'may not'表示可能性或让步。

points.structure: 让步转折结构 may not...but
points.explanation: 用may not承认前者的可能，再以but转折强调后者，语气委婉而坚定。
points.example_sentence: I may not fully master the art, but I have begun.
points.common_mistake: ❌ I may not fully master, but I began → ✅ I may not fully master, but I have begun (时态一致，强调开始的状态)
points.contrast: 与might not...but对比：might语气更不确定、更委婉；may较直接。
